specification.topic-statement: The status layer renders a 2-row display consisting of a horizontal separator line and the agent's current model identifier with thinking level.

scope.in-scope: Status visibility determination, fixed height reporting, and rendering of the separator and model/thinking-level content row.
scope.boundary: This topic provides three callbacks to the layer cake: visibility, height, and render.

This topic holds live references (raw pointers) to three fields in the agent: a boolean visibility flag, a nullable model string pointer, and an integer thinking-level. Changes to these fields are reflected immediately on the next render call; the layer does not cache copies.

behavior.visibility-check: Returns the current value of the agent's boolean visibility flag. The flag is initialized to true for all newly created agents.
behavior.height-reporting: Always returns 2 regardless of terminal width or any other input.
behavior.rendering:
The start_row and row_count parameters are ignored; both rows are always written unconditionally.
The light horizontal box-drawing character (─, 3 bytes UTF-8) is repeated for the full terminal width. An ANSI erase-to-end-of-line sequence and CRLF follow.
A robot emoji (🤖, 4 bytes UTF-8) followed by a space is appended first. Then a foreground color sequence for 256-color index 153 (soft blue) is generated.
If the agent's model pointer is null, the soft blue color sequence, the literal text "(no model)", and an ANSI color reset are appended.
If the agent's model pointer is non-null, the thinking-level integer is converted to a string using the mapping in the data contract above. The soft blue color sequence, the model string, a forward slash, the thinking-level string, and an ANSI color reset are appended. Example output: "claude-sonnet-4/medium".
An ANSI erase-to-end-of-line sequence and CRLF follow.
